zh.lifeSummary: 當Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager於大約1300年出生在西班牙阿拉格昂札拉格薩札拉格薩時，她的父親Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza27歲，母親Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera36歲。她於1314年與Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon在西班牙卡塔倫亞萊里達萊里達結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1327年10月20日在她的家鄉享年28歲，埋葬在西班牙阿拉格昂札拉格薩札拉格薩。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager于大约1300年出生在西班牙阿拉格昂札拉格薩札拉格薩时，她的父亲Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza27岁，母亲Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera36岁。她于1314年与Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon在西班牙卡塔倫亞萊里達萊里達结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1327年10月20日在她的家乡享年28岁，埋葬在西班牙阿拉格昂札拉格薩札拉格薩。

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Gravin
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Vito, Alfredo, Mario, Orlando, Rocco, Santo, Vincenza, Vincenzo.
Italian: habitational name from any of various places called Gravina, especially one in Apulia, near Bari.

variants.name: Gravino
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Dante, Angelo, Biagio, Gaetano, Guido, Lia, Marco, Rocco.
Italian: from a masculinized form of   Gravina  .

nameMeanings.name: van
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Anh, Thanh, Hung, Minh, Vinh, Dung, Hue, Huong, Lan, Quang, Tan, Thuy, Hong, Chi, Hang, Ho, Pok, Sang, Bai, Chin, Ching, Chu, Chuan, Dong.
Vietnamese (Vạn): from the Chinese surname 萬, see Wan 1.
Vietnamese (Văn): from the Chinese surnames 文 or 聞, see Wen 2 and 3, and also 5 below.

variants.name: Wan
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Hong, Yi, Qi, Qiang, Wai, Yiu, Yuk, Chien-Min, Fang, Hin, Jichun, Li, Man, Chung, Min, Yiping, Pak, Sik, Sung Man.  Vietnamese Tin, Bang, Da, Hien, Hoi, Lan, Minh Quang, Sau, Tich, Tung, Yaping, Yuet.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 萬, which means ‘ten thousand’ literally or ‘very great number’ figuratively: (i) from the personal name of Bi Wan (畢萬), an official in the state of Jin (located in present-day Shanxi province). He was said to be a descendant of Bi Gong Gao (known as Duke of Bi), the 15th son of the virtuous King Wen of Zhou (reined 1152–1056  BC ). Bi Wan was later granted the fiefdom of Wei (魏), which in 403  BC  became the state of Wei (located in present-day Shanxi, Shaanxi, Henan and Hebei provinces). Some of his descendants adopted Wei (魏) as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his personal name, as their surname. (ii) from the personal name Wan (萬), style name of Rui Bo (芮伯, the Earl of Rui), an official of the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ), who was granted the fief Rui (located in Ruicheng in Shaanxi province). Some of his descendants inherited Rui (芮), the name of the fief, as their surname, while others inherited 萬 (Wan), his style name, as their surname. (iii) from the placename Wan (萬), the name of a fief (located in Juxian in Shandong province) granted during the Spring and Autumn period (770–476  BC ) to an official in the ancient state of Chu. (iv) from the first element of the name of the Wan Si (萬俟) family, who lived in the downstream areas of the Yellow River during the Northern Qi dynasty (550–577  AD ). (v) from the second element of the name of the Tu Wan (吐萬) family, who are members of the Xianbei ethnic group in northern China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 完, meaning ‘complete’ in Chinese: (i) said to be borne by the descendants of Huan Zhui, a grandson of the Duke Huan of Song (ruler of the state of Song, died 651  BC ). (ii) from Wan (完), the name of Qu Wan (屈完), an official in the state of Chu during the Spring and Autumn Period (770–476  BC ). (iii) shortened form of the compound surname Wan-Yan (完顔), a surname from minority ethnic groups such as Juchen ethnic group in ancient northern China.

nameMeanings.name: Urgel
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Perhaps from German Gurgel ‘throat’, a nickname for a drinker.

nameMeanings.name: en
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 岑, referring to ‘small high hills’ or ‘precipice’ in Chinese: (i) from the placename Cen (岑), the name of a fief (located in Hancheng in present-day Shaanxi province), which was granted to Qu, nephew of King Wen of Zhou (1152–1056 BC ), by King Wu of Zhou (c. 1087–1043 BC ). (ii) for some families, this surname can be traced back to certain minority ethnic groups in ancient southern China.

nameMeanings.name: Ager
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (mainly Essex, Suffolk, and Northamptonshire): variant of   Alger   or possibly of   Edgar  .
Danish: habitational name from any of several places called Ager, meaning ‘plowed land’ (from a cognate of the English word acre).
French: from the ancient Germanic personal name Adgari, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + gari (from garwa) ‘ready, eager’ or gari ‘spear’. This surname is very rare in France.

variants.name: Agers
meanings: English: variant of Ager , with excrescent -s.
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Eggers  .

variants.name: Age
meanings: French (Agé): variant of Ager . Compare Agee .
English (Cornwall, Kent): probably a variant of Adge (with lengthened vowel), an apparently extinct name which is probably a local pronunciation of Hatch .
History: The surname Agé (see 1 above) is listed along with its altered form Agee in the (US) National Huguenot Society's register of qualified Huguenot ancestors.

variants.name: Alger
meanings: English: from the Middle English personal name Alger. Pronounced with a soft g it is from Old French Alg(i)er, Aug(i)er (ancient Germanic Adalgari, composed of the elements adal ‘noble’ + gāri ‘spear’); see Auger . Pronounced with a hard g, it is a variant of Algar .
Scottish and English: in Scotland, Alger may be a variant of Algeo .
German: from a shortened form of the ancient Germanic personal name Adalgar (see 1 above).

nameMeanings.name: Therese
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From Spanish. See Theresa .

en.lifeSummary: When Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager was born about 1300, in Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Spain, her father, Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza, was 27 and her mother, Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera, was 36. She married Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon in 1314, in Lleida, Lleida, Catalonia, Spain. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 20 October 1327, in her hometown, at the age of 28, and was buried in Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Spain.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager est née environ 1300, à Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Espagne, son père, Barón Gombau Entença d’Entença y Cabrera d’Enteza, avait 27 ans et sa mère, Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon en 1314, à Lleida, Lleida, Catalunya, Espagne. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 20 octobre 1327, dans sa ville natale, à l’âge de 28 ans, et a été enterrée à Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Espagne.

de.lifeSummary: Als Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager um 1300 in Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Spanien geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza 27 und ihre Mutter Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera 36. Sie hat 1314 in Lleida, Lleida, Catalunya, Spanien Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 20. Oktober 1327 im Alter von 28 Jahren in ihrem Geburtsort und wurde in Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Spanien begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager è nata il circa 1300 nel Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Spagna, sua padre, Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza, aveva 27 anni e sua madre, Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon nel 1314, nel Lleida, Lleida, Catalunya, Spagna. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 20 ottobre 1327, nel sua cittadina, all'età di 28 anni, e fu sepolta a Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Spagna.

ja.lifeSummary: Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Agerが約1300年にスペインアラグoンザラゴザザラゴザで生まれた時，父Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Entezaは27歳，母Constanza de Antillon y Cabreraは36歳であった。1314年にスペインカタルニーアレイダレイダでRey Don Alfonso IV de Aragonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1327年10月20日に故郷で28歳で死去し，スペインアラグoンザラゴザザラゴザにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager(이)가 대략 1300년 스페인 아라그o 자라고자 자라고자에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza(은)는 27세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1314년 스페인 카털니아 레이다 레이다에서 Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1327년 10월 20일 그녀의 고향에서 28세의 나이로 사망했으며 스페인 아라그o 자라고자 자라고자에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager nasceu aproximadamente 1300, em Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Espanha, seu pai, Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza, tinha 27 anos e sua mãe, Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon em 1314, em Lleida, Lleida, Catalunya, Espanha. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 20 de outubro de 1327, em sua cidade natal, com 28 anos, e foi sepultada em Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, Espanha.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager родилась около 1300 г. в местности зарагоза, зарагоза, арагoн, Испания, её отцу по имени Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza было 27 лет, а матери, которую звали Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon в 1314 г. в местности ллида, ллида, каталуня, Испания. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 20 октября 1327 г. в её родном городе в возрасте 28 лет и была похоронена в местности зарагоза, зарагоза, арагoн, Испания.

sm.desde 1314 hasta 1327: mai le 1314 e o'o I le 1327

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Dona Condesa Therese van Montpellier Gravin van Urgel en Ager nació aproximadamente 1300, en Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, España, su papá, Barón Gombau Entença de Entença y Cabrera de Enteza, tenía 27 años y su mamá, Constanza de Antillon y Cabrera, tenía 36 años. Se casó con Rey Don Alfonso IV de Aragon en 1314, en Lleida, Lérida, Cataluña, España. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 20 de octubre de 1327, en su pueblo, a la edad de 28 años, y fue sepultada en Zaragoza, Zaragoza, Aragón, España.
